Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Color adds an extra pop to any jewelry design. Designer and author Amee Sweet-McNamara blends her love of colors and shapes with soutache braid and tiny beads to create high-impact jewelry. Katie colors charms and pendants using permanent markers and alcohol inks.
Video.title: Make It Colorful

Video.summary: Angela is back for Season 5 of the Midnight Quilt Show! Today she is making the Arrows Quilt by Krystal Jakelwicz, and incorporating a little love note into her machine quilting.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: In the episode "Easy Projects with Native Materials," the focus is on crafting robust furniture using natural materials, bringing joy to woodworking enthusiasts. The project kicks off with a stool build using one-inch dowels for rails and sturdy legs crafted from one-and-a-half-inch square pieces. A standout feature is the woven seat, initially demonstrated with bundling twine but showcased with Cecil rope for its natural charm and durability. The construction process begins meticulously, drilling precise holes using a drill press for consistent alignment. Offset holes on adjacent faces accommodate the rails snugly, while chamfered ends prevent any interference during assembly. Dry-fitting ensures all components fit seamlessly before applying glue, guaranteeing a sturdy final product. After assembly, the stool is clamped securely to maintain square alignment and left to dry before sanding and finishing bring out the wood's natural beauty. The seat weaving process is a highlight, with the speaker demonstrating how to wrap Cecil rope around the frame in a precise pattern, creating a durable and visually appealing seat. Throughout, the importance of precision and the handy use of pliers for control during weaving are emphasized. The episode also explores comparisons between stools made from different materials, celebrating the diversity of woodworking choices. Transitioning to a coffee table project, the speaker selects wood pieces with a striking blue stain for added visual interest. Techniques for drilling and joint fitting are expertly demonstrated, ensuring the table frame is not just sturdy but also artistically crafted. Challenges encountered, such as uneven surfaces or knots in the tabletop, are met with practical solutions, including finishing tips with Danish oil for a natural, lustrous look. Throughout the tutorial, the joy of working with native materials and the satisfaction of creating beautiful, enduring furniture pieces shine through, making it a delightful watch for woodworking enthusiasts of all skill levels.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Nature-Inspired Crafts: Wooden Rope Seat & Slab Wood Coffee Table

Image.alt: Nature-Inspired Crafts: Wooden Rope Seat & Slab Wood Coffee Table
Nature-Inspired Crafts: Wooden Rope Seat & Slab Wood Coffee Table
Nature-Inspired Crafts: Wooden Rope Seat & Slab Wood Coffee Table

Video.summary: George Vondriska is in the shop to show you how to make your own hand-turned bottle stoppers and pizza cutters. Whether they're a gift for a loved one or just something to show off in your own kitchen, these pieces are easier to make than you might think. He'll also show you how to make your own saw horses and stools to give you more real estate in the shop.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Handmade Bottle Stoppers, Pizza Cutters, Saw Horses, & Shop Stools

Image.alt: Handmade Bottle Stoppers, Pizza Cutters, Saw Horses, & Shop Stools
Handmade Bottle Stoppers, Pizza Cutters, Saw Horses, & Shop Stools
Handmade Bottle Stoppers, Pizza Cutters, Saw Horses, & Shop Stools

Video.summary: Control is not the first word that comes to mind when thinking of modern quilting, but there are some techniques that can benefit by keeping things under control. Catherine Cureton illustrates the idea of controlled scrappy quilting. She has a new method for sorting fabrics that allows for stress free scrappy quilting. Then, Audrey Esarey has the guide for sewing curves. Keep stitching under control with some simple tips for curves. Then it's tips for walking foot quilting with Eliane Bergmann in the machine tip.
Video.title: Under Control

Video.summary: Angela is back! In addition to quilting an amazing half-square triangle quilt by Monique Dillard, she's also announcing something HUGE!
Video.title: Night Sky Quilt

Video.summary: This week Angela is preparing to host a dinner for quilters, so of course, she has to quilt up a few placemats!
Video.title: Quilt-As-You-Go Placemats

Video.summary: Angela’s not the only Midnight Quilter in the Walters' house! Watch as she and her teenage daughter Cloe spend some quality time together making an easy-to-piece charm quilt.
Video.title: Like a Charm

Video.summary: Believe it or not, this quilt is only made of straight seams! Watch it "unfold" as Angela conquers the Strip City pattern with Boundless blues.
Video.title: Strip City

Video.summary: It was only a matter of time before the Midnight Quilt Show tackled Janice Ryan's Wallflower Quilt! You bring the wine, Angela's got the Flying Geese covered.
Video.title: Wallflower Variable Star Quilt

Video.summary: It was only a matter of time before the Midnight Quilt Show tackled Janice Ryan's Wallflower Quilt! You bring the wine, Angela's got the Flying Geese covered.
Video.title: Wallflower Variable Star Quilt

Video.summary: It seems like only yesterday that Angela was stitching a baby quilt for her youngest daughter. Tonight, she's making a flower quilt for her daughter's new big girl bed. Power to the flower!
Video.title: Flower Power Kids Quilt

Video.summary: Wish you could make your quilting mistakes disappear? Angela is here to show you how. Well, at least how to hide them by using a matching thread color, echoing and repeating a mistake to make it look intentional.
Video.title: Bonus: Hide Your Quilting Mistakes

Video.summary: Can’t get enough of bargello? Angela Walters shows you how to make a ravishing rainbow quilt using precut fabric strip sets.
Video.title: Bargello Beauty Rainbow Quilt

Video.summary: Angela's reminded of her age as she relies on her kids to keep up with the digital world. Watch as she uses two basic shapes to create a mod half-square triangle quilt that's easier to make than it looks.
Video.title: Modern Technology Half-Square Triangle Quilt

Video.summary: Sometimes bulky seams can't be avoided, but DON'T panic! Angela has a few tips for how to deal with this quilting conundrum.
Video.title: Bonus: Tips for Avoiding Bulky Seams

Image.alt: Bonus: Tips for Avoiding Bulky Seams
Bonus: Tips for Avoiding Bulky Seams
Bonus: Tips for Avoiding Bulky Seams

Video.summary: Angela's back and bursting with love. In this season premiere, she uses half-square triangles to make a modern bursting star quilt while sharing her tips on how to keep a lasting relationship with a quilter.
Video.title: Modern Bursting Star Quilt

Video.summary: Wish you could make your quilting mistakes disappear? Angela is here to show you how. Well, at least how to hide them by using a matching thread color, echoing and repeating a mistake to make it look intentional.
Video.title: Bonus: Hide Your Quilting Mistakes

Video.summary: During World War II, feminine fashion took on a more masculine look. But women added feminine touches. Joann Banko creates embroidered lingerie. Then Heather Valentine shows how to top off boots with a lacey cuff. Joanne Banko adds beads and trims with the pearls and sequin foot.
Video.title: Feminine Fashions in Wartime - 1940's

Video.summary: Blogger Andrew Thornton talks about drawing inspiration from your art and turning your hobby into a business. Then, he shares one of his favorite new jewelry making techniques on making decorative end caps using epoxy clay. Katie's Beading Lesson is about using beaded chain for an easy necklace.
Video.title: Art Inspired Jewelry Making

Video.summary: Making art is a process that requires start to finish instructions. Tammy Honaman is up first with the basics of bead embroidery – what you need, the tools, and various finishes and shapes. Then meet Katie Baum as she takes you through a sterling ring from start to finish - learn about kilns and working in sterling.
Video.title: The Artistic Process

Video.summary: If one page is good, a whole bunch is better. This show focuses on books. First, host Julie Fei-Fan Balzer uses colorful paint pens to create an art journal page of doodled leaves. Next, Joe Rotella shares secrets of book binding demonstrating a piano hinge style binding. Then, Stacy Julian makes a treasures theme album with gold glittery elements. Last, Julie uses an electronic cutter to make numerous triangle shapes to embellish a cover for an album.
Video.title: Episode 5: Books

Video.summary: New materials are the spice to your scrapbook soup. Every dish needs a little dash of pepper to add some flavor. It'sthe same with scrapbooking - bland is not very interesting. Fill in the spaces of your letters with paper on the "attitudes in generosity" page, build on a core to add dimension with Stacey Caron for a cherished layout, and projects featuring paper, acetate and glass from Sara Naumann; a spicy mix today on Scrapbook Soup.
Video.title: Different Materials

Video.summary: Get out those paints and brushes and let's paint! Collage artist Rebekah Meier uses custom-cut stencils, masking tape and paints to create a cool canvas. Dina Wakley uses thick and thin paint methods to make an artistic journal page. Host Julie Fei Fan Balzer works with additive and reductive mono-printing techniques. In the tip, Seth Apter shares innovative embossing ideas.
Video.title: Paint Technique

Video.summary: The first step in creating the make it artsy lifestyle is starting with daily activity. By reinforcing art daily, we develop a behavior and routine to being creative. Host Julie Fei Fan Balzer begins with a daily calendar, and then Jodi Ohl introduces us to her routine of coffee and watching the birds in her yard. Finally, artist Tori Weyers introduces us to a daily sketchbook practice. You can always find time for a simple artful expression.
Video.title: Episode 1: Daily Activities

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Baking & Cooking

Video.summary: The ladies are going big by thinking small! From compact cables to cute mini skeins, this one's all about the tiny treasures that keep those needles clicking.
Video.title: Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact

Image.alt: Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact
Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact
Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact

Video.summary: Beat single sock syndrome with a little multitasking! The girls go over how to knit two socks at once so you'll never end up with solo socks again.
Video.title: Multitasking for Knitters: Two-at-a-Time Socks

Video.summary: We create some new locations for creative play as we change the setting around us. We start out with a tree that reflects the changing seasons. Then, we'll make a play tent for imaginary adventures. The next page of our art journal is a surreal collage from magazine cutouts. Last, we create a portable playscape of the forest.
Video.title: Settings

Video.summary: Travel to the future for new adventures. Jenny Barnett-Rohrs makes a metallic robot tray to hold coins and treasures. Then, she creates a mysterious sea creature. Franz Spohn launches aliens into outer space and Jenny makes futuristic sculptures from foam.
Video.title: Sci-Fi

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Cook & Create

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
